Jesse Miller, Esq. (415.543.8700; jessemiller@reedsmith.com) of Reed Smith LLP for Plaintiff, First Student, Inc.
Chris Braham, Esq. (310.277.4110; cbraham@mwe.com) of McDermott Will & Emery LLP for Third Party, Durham School Services, LP.

No appearance by counsel for Defendant Mark Frith who filed the motion for protective order to prohibit inspection of Yahoo email account. All parties stated the motion for protective to prohibit inspection of Yahoo email account is not contested.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARK FRITH's Motion For Protective Order To Prohibit Inspection Of Yahoo Email Account:
The Motion is granted as follows: The parties shall jointly retain a neutral third-party vendor, like TLS or a different professional computer forensic consultant other than FSI's agent, A&M, - at FSI's sole expense - to obtain a forensic copy of the Yahoo Account, perform an inspection pursuant to an order to be issued by the Court, and retain possession of the copy of the Yahoo Account to preserve Frith's and Frith's counsel's respective attorney-client privilege and work product protection claims and to ensure that Frith's privacy interests are adequately protected. Frith's counsel shall prepare a proposed order for inspection and provide it to FSI for comments. If there is a dispute as to the content of the order, the parties shall meet and confer in person or virtually (e.g. Zoom) before submitting the order to the court for approval.

Order is signed.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFF FIRST STUDENT, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Defendants Vivek Garg And Zum Services, Inc. To Produce Devices For Inspection.

Plaintiff's motion to compel defendants Vivek Garg and Zum Services, Inc. to produce devices for inspection is granted. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted.

Defendants Garg and Zum shall produce and permit plaintiff and its consultants to inspect each computing device (e.g., laptop, desktop, tablet) used by Garg regarding his employment at Zum, including but not limited to the Apple MacBook laptop computer identified in paragraph 5 of Garg's November 4, 2021 declaration. The computing device or devices shall be produced for inspection within 10 days of this order or at such other time as the parties mutually agree so as to minimize disruption of defendants' ability to conduct business.

Order is signed.

Emily Lynch, Esq. (415.543.8700; elynch@reedsmith.com) for Plaintiff.

DEFENDANTS ZUM SERVICES, INC. and VIVEK GARG's Motion To Strike Portions Of The Third Amended Complaint.

Defendants' motion to strike is denied. This motion largely repeats the arguments raised in the demurrer. For the reasons explained in the companion ruling (e.g. plaintiff can allege violation of the AUP), the motion to strike is denied.

One order signed for demurrer and motion to strike. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported.

Defendant Zum Services, Inc., Vivek Garg Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily Lynch, Esq. (415.543.8700; elynch@reedsmith.com) for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ZUM SERVICES, INC. AND VIVEK GARG'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT:

Defendants' demurrer is overruled.

The third cause of action for violation of the UCL is pleaded with the requisite particularity. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 619.) The court liberally reads the complaint pursuant to CCP 452. Reading the complaint as a whole, plaintiff sufficiently alleges an unlawful business practice based upon the violation of Penal Code sec. 502. Plaintiff also sufficiently alleges an "unfair" business practice. Plaintiff's bid sabotage allegations clearly allege anti-competitive conduct. (See Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") pars. 74, 83-91, 111; Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 163, 187 ["the word 'unfair' in [Section 17200] means conduct that threatens an incipient violation of an antitrust law, or violates the policy or spirit of one of those laws because its effects are comparable to or the same as a violation of the law, or otherwise significantly threatens or harms competition."].) Plaintiff also sufficiently alleges a fraudulent business practice because the described conduct was likely to deceive the public. (See Tucker v. Pac. Bell Mobile Servs. (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 201, 225-226.)

Plaintiff's eighth cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations states a claim. The elements are: (1) a valid contract between plaintiff and a third party; (2) defendant's knowledge of this contract; (3) defendant's intentional acts designed to induce a breach or disruption of the contractual relationship; (4) actual breach or disruption of the contractual relationship; and (5) resulting damage. (See Pacific Gas & Electric Co. v. Bear Stearns & Co. (1990) 50 Cal.3d 1118, 1126.) First, the claim can be predicated on the parties' confidentiality agreement. Defendant cannot demurrer to part of a cause of action. (See Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856, fn.14.) Second, at this stage of the proceedings, the court cannot conclude as a matter of law, that the Acceptable Use Policy ("AUP") is not a binding covenant. On demurrer, the court will generally defer to plaintiff's construction of an agreement where the instrument is ambiguous and reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation. (See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Here, a factual issue remains regarding whether the terms of the handbook are binding on the parties in light of the disclaimer and other language of the handbook. (See Defendants' RJN, Ex. A FS000298 ["I acknowledge that I am expected to read, understand and adhere to the Company policies and will familiarize myself with the material in the Employee Handbook."]; FS000234 [disclaimer].) Plaintiff also sufficiently alleges the ultimate fact of defendants' knowledge of the agreement. (See TAC, pars. 195-196; Jenni Rivera Enterprises, LLC v. Latin World Entertainment Holdings, Inc. (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 766, 783 [full knowledge of contractual terms is not necessary to establish intentional interference with contractual relations tort].)

One order signed for demurrer and motion to strike.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)

Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendant Mark Friths June 1 Opposition (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Motion To Uphold The Confidentiality Designation And Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. JESSE L. MILLER, Esq. and REBECCA G. DURHAM, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (Tel: 415-659-5973) for plaintiff First Student, Inc.
Atty. PETER H. BALES, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 415-227-0900) for defendant Mark Frith.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion To Uphold The Confidentiality Designation And Motion To Seal.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted. The Motion is primarily focused on the "SFUSD Narrative." In his June 9, 2029 Order, Judge Schulman held that the Narrative is "reasonably described as [a] trade secret." Order 5:19. Moreover, the dispute arises in the context of discovery, and as such the Narrative is not subject to the sealing requirements of CRC 2.550; 2.551.

Accordingly, the following documents are HEREBY SEALED in the manner outline below:

(1) The entirety of Exhibit 5 to Declaration of Julian Mack In Opposition To Plaintiff's
Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal ("Mack Declaration")
(2) Numbered pages 29-31 of Exhibit 30 to the Mack Declaration;
(3) The references to the SFUSD Narrative in the June 1, 2021 UNREDACTED -
Opposition To Plaintiff's Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal, as outlined in Frith's publicly filed REDACTED version of this document; and
(4) The references to the SFUSD Narrative in the June 1, 2021 UNREDACTED
Appendix to Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal, as outlined in Frith's publicly filed REDACTED version of this document.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR No. 13293, Tel: 415-274-9977, Email: calendar-la@veritext.com. (302/JPT)

Atty. PETER H. BALES, Esq. of Buchalter (Tel: 415-227-0900) for defendant Mark Frith.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF FIRST STUDENT, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION's Motion To Seal Portions Of Defendant Mark Frith's June 1 Opposition.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted. The Motion is primarily focused on the "SFUSD Narrative." In his June 9, 2029 Order, Judge Schulman held that the Narrative is "reasonably described as [a] trade secret." Order 5:19. Moreover, the dispute arises in the context of discovery, and as such the Narrative is not subject to the sealing requirements of CRC 2.550; 2.551.

Accordingly, the following documents are HEREBY SEALED in the manner outline below:

(1) The entirety of Exhibit 5 to Declaration of Julian Mack In Opposition To Plaintiff's
Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal ("Mack Declaration")
(2) Numbered pages 29-31 of Exhibit 30 to the Mack Declaration;
(3) The references to the SFUSD Narrative in the June 1, 2021 UNREDACTED -
Opposition To Plaintiff's Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal, as outlined in Frith's publicly filed REDACTED version of this document; and
(4) The references to the SFUSD Narrative in the June 1, 2021 UNREDACTED
Appendix to Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion to Uphold Confidentiality Designation and Motion to Seal, as outlined in Frith's publicly filed REDACTED version of this document.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR No. 13293, Tel: 415-274-9977, Email: calendar-la@veritext.com. (302/JPT)
